coverageNotes: A 5,000 sq ft product covers about 61% of the full lawn.
Two 5,000 sq ft units are typically needed for a full-property application.

zones.name: Front Main Lawn
zones.strategy: Main front lawn area. Use this as the primary front-yard turf reference.
recommendations: Prioritize clean mowing and visible turf quality.
Plan product coverage against the 8,225 sq ft full-lawn total unless intentionally spot-treating.

zones.name: Front Side Lawn
zones.strategy: Side-front turf on both west and east sides of house.
recommendations: Watch edge stress and sprinkler coverage differences.
Use Zone 1 runtime context when diagnosing dryness.

zones.name: Back Main Lawn
recommendations: Check coverage before summer fertilizer decisions.
Use this area as the main backyard turf reference.

zones.name: Back Small Lawn
recommendations: Watch west-side heat and watering behavior.
Consider this separately from the larger back main lawn for repair decisions.

landscape.label: Backyard shrubs / bushes
certaintyNotes: Juniper "Kettle" is a soft identification and should not be treated as fully certain.

notes: Some plant and installation details are approximate.
Exact counts are not important for plant references right now.
